name: Mestanza Three Passes Loop: Madrona, Los Rehoyos & El Calero
description: Cycle the difficult Mestanza Three Passes Loop, a 50.7-mile route with 5792 feet of gain through Sierra Madrona's scenic passes.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Mestanza are situated within the Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona Natural Park, offering diverse landscapes for cyclists. The region features expansive plains dotted with ancient oak trees in the Valle de Alcudia, contrasting with the rugged relief and dramatic ridges of the Sierra Madrona mountain range. Rivers and streams wind through these areas, and the prominent Pantano del Montoro (Montoro Reservoir) is a notable natural feature. The terrain includes both well-paved surfaces and some unpaved sections, providing varied cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Mestanza The most popular touring cycling route is Mestanza Three Passes Loop: Madrona, Los Rehoyos & El Calero, a 50.7 miles (81.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 53 minutes to complete. This difficult route involves significant elevation gain while traversing the scenic passes of Sierra Madrona. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Hinojosas de Calatrava and Almod, a difficult 36.8 miles (59.2 km) path. This route connects two towns, offering varied landscapes and a challenging ride through the region's natural beauty. Local touring cyclists also love the Montoro and Tablillas Reservoirs Loop, a 27.3 miles (44.0 km) trail leading through reservoir areas, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. Touring cycling around Mestanza is defined by expansive plains, rugged mountain ranges, and prominent reservoirs. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Mestanza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mestanza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Mestanza?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;over 10 touring cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Mestanza, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are situated within the diverse landscapes of the Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona Natural Park.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Mestanza?
A: The routes around Mestanza cater to various abilities. You'll find &lt;strong&gt;3 easy routes&lt;/strong&gt;, &lt;strong&gt;2 moderate routes&lt;/strong&gt;, and &lt;strong&gt;8 difficult routes&lt;/strong&gt;, ensuring options for beginners, intermediate riders, and experienced cyclists seeking a challenge.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Mestanza?
A: Optimal cycling conditions in Mestanza are generally found in &lt;strong&gt;spring and autumn&lt;/strong&gt;. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, making for a more comfortable and enjoyable ride through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, for those seeking a less strenuous ride, there are easy and moderate options. A good choice for a moderate, shorter ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertollano – Puertollano loop from Puertollano&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 20.2 km and takes around 1 hour 28 minutes.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Mestanza is known for its challenging routes, particularly within the Sierra Madrona. For a demanding experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375060390/tablillas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tablillas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 38.2 km route with significant elevation changes, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374615022/hinojosas-de-calatrava-and-almod" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hinojosas de Calatrava and Almod&lt;/a&gt; route, which is 59.2 km and also rated difficult.

Q: What natural features or attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the &lt;strong&gt;Pantano del Montoro&lt;/strong&gt; (Montoro Reservoir), a prominent natural feature. You might also encounter expansive plains with ancient oak trees in the Valle de Alcudia, or dramatic ridges in the Sierra Madrona. The region is part of a natural park, so keep an eye out for diverse flora and fauna.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Mestanza?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058415241/montoro-and-tablillas-reservoirs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montoro and Tablillas Reservoirs Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option, covering 44 km. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375060390/tablillas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tablillas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about cycling in Mestanza?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.3 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from over 18 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from serene plains to rugged mountains, and the scenic views, especially around the reservoirs. The variety of routes catering to different skill levels is also frequently highlighted.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona Natural Park is a vital sanctuary for numerous species. While cycling, you might catch glimpses of emblematic wildlife such as the &lt;strong&gt;Iberian lynx&lt;/strong&gt;, the &lt;strong&gt;Iberian imperial eagle&lt;/strong&gt;, and the &lt;strong&gt;black vulture&lt;/strong&gt;, among others. The well-preserved Mediterranean forest also supports diverse flora and fauna.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Mestanza and its surroundings offer cultural and historical sites. You can find historic buildings like the 16th-century Church of San Esteban Protomártir and the 14th-century Hermitage of the Virgen de la Antigua. The &lt;strong&gt;Fuente Agria&lt;/strong&gt;, a historic mineral-medicinal spring, is also a notable point of interest, and the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5460757" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertollano&lt;/a&gt; offers additional attractions like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7199651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo de San Gregorio&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7199672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doctor Fleming Square&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Mestanza's touring cycling routes?
A: The terrain around Mestanza is quite varied. You'll find a mix of &lt;strong&gt;well-paved surfaces&lt;/strong&gt;, suitable for road cycling, particularly in the Valle de Alcudia's expansive plains. However, some routes, especially in the Sierra Madrona, incorporate &lt;strong&gt;unpaved sections&lt;/strong&gt;, adding an element of adventure and requiring appropriate bike choice.
tips: Montoro Reservoir is a sanitary reservoir built in 1950 that regulates the waters of the Montoro River. Road in good condition.

Magnificent views of the Sierra de la Solana. The climb can be tough at times. Looking west there is a dirt road that will take you to the Los Rehoyos viewpoint - do not hesitate to go there. It is about 400m to be able to enjoy the even more impressive views: the Alcudia valley, the Montoro reservoir, Mestanza and its mountains, the Diogenes Mines, Hinojosas de Calatrava, the Alberquilla volcanic lagoon... and on the other side, views towards the Sierra Madrona, Cuevas Altas, the Sierra ALcoba, the Robledillo Valley and the peaks of the Sierra de la Solana.

Beautiful port, very quiet area with almost no circulation of motor vehicles, the road and the state of the road is perfect.

This port on the south side is very bearable and without steep slopes, very constant at 3/4%.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to cycle the Mestanza Three Passes Loop?
faqs.answer: This challenging touring bicycle route is estimated to take around 5 hours and 53 minutes to complete. This duration is for the cycling time itself and does not include stops for rest, food, or sightseeing.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult. With significant elevation changes and multiple mountain passes, it is best suited for intermediate to experienced touring cyclists who are comfortable with substantial climbs and descents.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is a loop, and while there isn't a single official trailhead, Mestanza is a nearby town and a logical starting point. You would typically find parking within Mestanza or at suitable points near the beginning of the loop, depending on local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly on paved roads, making it suitable for touring bikes. However, as it traverses the Sierra Madrona region, expect a mountainous profile with significant elevation changes, including three distinct mountain passes.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect natural beauty characteristic of the Sierra Madrona region. Cycling through the passes will offer expansive views of the surrounding mountains and valleys, providing a remote and immersive experience in nature.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in the Sierra Madrona region, and thus this route, is generally during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). Temperatures are milder, and the weather is usually more stable, making the significant climbs more enjoyable than in the heat of summer.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features several notable points. You'll pass by the Montoro Reservoir Bridge, and cycle over the mountain passes of Puerto Los Rehoyos and Puerto Madrona, which offer panoramic views.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with official cycling trails. You will encounter parts of the [CIMA CR02] El Calero, [CIMA CR05] Los Rehoyos, and [CIMA CR06] Madrona trails, among others.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in natural areas, this route is designed for touring bicycles on paved roads with significant climbs. It's not typically suitable for cycling with dogs unless they are in a dedicated trailer, and you should always ensure they are on a leash when off the bike and respect local wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for cycling in the Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, cycling on established roads and trails within the Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona Natural Park does not require specific permits or entrance fees for individuals. However, always check the latest regulations from the park authorities before your visit, especially for large groups or organized events.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food or water along the route?
faqs.answer: Given the remote nature of parts of the Sierra Madrona, amenities like cafes or water sources may be sparse, especially between passes. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration of the ride. Mestanza, as a nearby town, would be the most reliable place for supplies before and after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I consider packing for this challenging cycling route?
faqs.answer: For this difficult touring cycling route, pack essentials like plenty of water, energy-rich snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), appropriate cycling attire for varying weather conditions, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. A reliable GPS device with the route downloaded is also crucial due to the potentially remote areas.
